skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem: The function f is defined by f(x) = −(1/3)(x − 6)² + 12, and the function g is defined by g(x) = −x + 12. What is one possible value of a such that f(a) = g(a)?
rationale: Setting f(a) = g(a): −(1/3)(a − 6)² + 12 = −a + 12. Subtracting 12 from both sides: −(1/3)(a − 6)² = −a. Multiplying by −3: (a − 6)² = 3a. Expanding: a² − 12a + 36 = 3a, so a² − 15a + 36 = 0. Factoring: (a − 3)(a − 12) = 0, so a = 3 or a = 12.
